source.input_excerpt: A clinic intake coordinator is reviewing administrative readiness for a new appointment request. The request says to build from the intake_packet data model, eligibility API status, document fields, JSON schema, prompt template, tool call r

activity_model.activity: Deciding whether the packet is ready to schedule, should be returned for missing consent, or needs insurance verification escalation
activity_model.objective: Whether the packet is ready to schedule, should be returned for missing consent, or needs insurance verification escalation
outcomes: An intake handoff with the next administrative action and reason
domain_vocabulary: administrative readiness for a new appointment request

interaction_contract.primary_decision: Whether the packet is ready to schedule, should be returned for missing consent, or needs insurance verification escalation
next_actions: The outcome is an intake handoff with the next administrative action and reason
Use domain terms: intake packet, referral, insurance verification, signed consent, appointment slot, administrative handoff
interaction_contract.completion: An intake handoff with the next administrative action and reason
make_easy: Confirm the activity model before screen structure
Review the primary decision and outcome in domain language
Adjust vocabulary before implementation detail reaches the primary UI

steps: Review referral, insurance, and required forms
Choose schedule, return for forms, or insurance verification
decision_points: Decide whether the packet is administratively ready, missing required consent, or needs insurance verification
workflow.completion_state: The intake team receives the next administrative action and reason

user_facing_terms: administrative readiness for a new appointment request

handoff.reason: Signed consent is missing before the appointment can be scheduled
handoff.next_action: Return the packet for consent and keep the request in administrative review

disclosure_reminders.primary_ui_rule: Keep implementation details and JudgmentKit review-packet terms out of product UI.

evidence: complete desktop and mobile browser QA

implementation_contract.purpose: Give agents an enforceable implementation gate after activity/workflow judgment, so generated UI uses approved project primitives instead of ad hoc controls
authority_order: repo-local interface contract and helper primitives
external product UI system evidence when explicitly provided

required_evidence: controls preserve target size and spacing
validation and disabled reasons are readable

default_rules: feature render paths must not emit raw input, select, textarea, or checkbox markup outside approved helpers
new primitives or variants require updating the repo-local contract first
generated UI must include a local check command or equivalent static inspection

checks: keyboard focus check for interactive controls
overflow check for long labels, descriptions, and values
modal or dialog footer order check when dialogs are present
disabled, empty, error, and ready state review

failure_signals: raw form controls appear in feature render code
checkboxes or labels rely on browser defaults without approved spacing
a new variant is invented without contract evidence
non-destructive modal or dialog actions put cancel/dismiss after the primary completion action
focus, disabled, or error states are missing
browser QA is skipped after UI generation
